behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus tool parsing/aggregation across module handoff (git subprocess -> parser -> report). MOCK-MISS: a unit parser test can pass while real `git` emits rename metadata/threshold behavior that changes classification. REAL-INFRA is required (real repo + real git subprocess/index semantics). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only git change categorization, with no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit baseline, rename a file (`git mv`), run the CLI against that repo, assert report shows rename count/file entry and does not increment added/deleted for the same path.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real binary diff output and subprocess behavior from git; mocks can hide crash cases triggered by actual binary patch metadata/encoding. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both point to System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about git/binary diff handling only, not model output or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in the correct count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD/no-commit edge cases are defined by real git command behavior and exit codes, so subprocess+repo state is the true failure boundary. Integration with mocked git responses can miss real stderr/exit handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies repository commit state handling only; no AI/ML or multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/non-error exit and structured empty report on stdout (not exception text).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history semantics (adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits), which depend on real git history traversal and subprocess output. MOCK-MISS: stubbing history can trivially mirror expected counts and miss real command/query mismatches. Rejected higher levels: behavior text concerns git history accounting only, not AI/ML judgments or chained agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI for the target range, and assert each reported category count matches the ground-truth commit operations.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior is governed by real git index/working-tree mechanics and diff command semantics; in-process fakes often flatten these states and hide mismatches. REAL-INFRA requires exercising actual git index transitions. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic CLI/git state comparison with no model-dependent output and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both conditions, and assert structured stdout output is byte-for-byte (or schema-for-schema) identical.
